-lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,280p' models/codex/quota.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"from __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport json\\\\nimport urllib.error\\\\nimport urllib.request\\\\nfrom concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed\\\\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\\\\nfrom typing import Any, List\\\\n\\\\nfrom .account import CodexAccount\\\\n\\\\nUSAGE_URL = \\\\\\\"https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass QuotaError(RuntimeError):\\\\n    def __init__(self, message: str, status_code: int | None = None) -> None:\\\\n        super().__init__(message)\\\\n        self.status_code = status_code\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n@dataclass\\\\nclass CodexQuota:\\\\n    email: str\\\\n    plan_type: str\\\\n    weekly_used_percent: int\\\\n    weekly_reset_at: int\\\\n    weekly_limit_reached: bool\\\\n    burst_used_percent: int\\\\n    burst_reset_at: int\\\\n    code_review_used_percent: int\\\\n    code_review_reset_at: int\\\\n    raw: dict | None = None\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _get_int(value: Any, default: int = 0) -> int:\\\\n    try:\\\\n        return int(value)\\\\n    except (TypeError, ValueError):\\\\n        return default\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef fetch_quota(access_token: str, account_id: str) -> CodexQuota:\\\\n    req = urllib.request.Request(USAGE_URL, method=\\\\\\\"GET\\\\\\\")\\\\n    req.add_header(\\\\\\\"Authorization\\\\\\\", f\\\\\\\"Bearer {access_token}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    if account_id:\\\\n        req.add_header(\\\\\\\"ChatGPT-Account-Id\\\\\\\", account_id)\\\\n    req.add_header(\\\\\\\"User-Agent\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"CodexBar\\\\\\\")\\\\n    req.add_header(\\\\\\\"Accept\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"application/json\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n    try:\\\\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=20) as resp:\\\\n            payload = resp.read().decode(\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\")\\\\n    except urllib.error.HTTPError as exc:\\\\n        body = exc.read().decode(\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\", errors=\\\\\\\"ignore\\\\\\\")\\\\n        raise QuotaError(f\\\\\\\"Quota fetch failed: HTTP {exc.code} {body}\\\\\\\", status_code=exc.code) from exc\\\\n    except urllib.error.URLError as exc:\\\\n        raise QuotaError(f\\\\\\\"Quota fetch failed: {exc.reason}\\\\\\\") from exc\\\\n\\\\n    try:\\\\n        data = json.loads(payload)\\\\n    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:\\\\n        raise QuotaError(\\\\\\\"Quota response was not valid JSON\\\\\\\") from exc\\\\n\\\\n    rate_limit = data.get(\\\\\\\"rate_limit\\\\\\\") or {}\\\\n    primary = rate_limit.get(\\\\\\\"primary_window\\\\\\\") or {}\\\\n    secondary = rate_limit.get(\\\\\\\"secondary_window\\\\\\\") or {}\\\\n\\\\n    code_review = data.get(\\\\\\\"code_review_rate_limit\\\\\\\") or {}\\\\n    code_primary = code_review.get(\\\\\\\"primary_window\\\\\\\") or {}\\\\n\\\\n    return CodexQuota(\\\\n        email=data.get(\\\\\\\"email\\\\\\\") or \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n        plan_type=data.get(\\\\\\\"plan_type\\\\\\\") or \\\\\\\"unknown\\\\\\\",\\\\n        weekly_used_percent=_get_int(primary.get(\\\\\\\"used_percent\\\\\\\")),\\\\n        weekly_reset_at=_get_int(primary.get(\\\\\\\"reset_at\\\\\\\")),\\\\n        weekly_limit_reached=bool(rate_limit.get(\\\\\\\"limit_reached\\\\\\\", False)),\\\\n        burst_used_percent=_get_int(secondary.get(\\\\\\\"used_percent\\\\\\\")),\\\\n        burst_reset_at=_get_int(secondary.get(\\\\\\\"reset_at\\\\\\\")),\\\\n        code_review_used_percent=_get_int(code_primary.get(\\\\\\\"used_percent\\\\\\\")),\\\\n        code_review_reset_at=_get_int(code_primary.get(\\\\\\\"reset_at\\\\\\\")),\\\\n        raw=data,\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef fetch_all_quotas(accounts: List[CodexAccount]) -> List[CodexQuota]:\\\\n    if not accounts:\\\\n        return []\\\\n\\\\n    results: List[CodexQuota] = []\\\\n    errors = []\\\\n\\\\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=min(8, len(accounts))) as executor:\\\\n        futures = {\\\\n            executor.submit(fetch_quota, account.access_token, account.account_id): account\\\\n            for account in accounts\\\\n        }\\\\n        for future in as_completed(futures):\\\\n            account = futures[future]\\\\n            try:\\\\n                results.append(future.result())\\\\n            except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - pass-through\\\\n                errors.append((account.email, exc))\\\\n\\\\n    if errors:\\\\n        details = \\\\\\\"; \\\\\\\".join(f\\\\\\\"{email}: {exc}\\\\\\\" for email, exc in errors)\\\\n        raise QuotaError(f\\\\\\\"Failed to fetch quota for some accounts: {details}\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n    return results\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_11\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash str\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nRunner = Callable[[list[str], str], Awaitable[CommandExecution]]\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass StageExecutionError(RuntimeError):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Base stage execution error.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass QuotaExhaustedError(StageExecutionError):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Raised when the underlying provider reports quota exhaustion.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, provider: str, message: str, partial_output: str = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\") -> None:\\\\n        super().__init__(message)\\\\n        self.provider = provider\\\\n        self.partial_output = partial_output\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass PermissionBlockedError(StageExecutionError):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Raised when an action is blocked by the permission gate.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, command: str, decision: PermissionDecision) -> None:\\\\n        super().__init__(decision.reason)\\\\n        self.command = command\\\\n        self.decision = decision\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass StageExecutor:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Execute planned stages via CLI backends or lightweight API calls.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    APPROVED_DECISIONS = {\\\\\\\"auto_approved\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"model_approved\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"user_approved\\\\\\\"}\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        *,\\\\n        chat_client: Optional[Chat2APIClient] = None,\\\\n        permission_gate: Optional[PermissionGate] = None,\\\\n        runner: Optional[Runner] = None,\\\\n        codex_tier: str = settings.CODEX_TIER,\\\\n    ) -> None:\\\\n        self.chat_client = chat_client or Chat2APIClient()\\\\n        self.permission_gate = permission_gate or PermissionGate()\\\\n        self.runner = runner or self._run_command\\\\n        self.codex_tier = codex_tier\\\\n\\\\n    async def execute(self, stage: StageRecord, handoff_doc_path: str, working_dir: str | None) -> StageExecutionResult:\\\\n        started_at = time.time()\\\\n        prompt = Path(handoff_doc_path).read_text(encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\")\\\\n        provider = stage.assigned_provider or self._infer_provider(stage.assigned_model or \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\")\\\\n        cwd = working_dir or \\\\\\\".\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n        if provider == \\\\\\\"chat2api\\\\\\\":\\\\n            raw_output = await asyncio.to_thread(\\\\n                self.chat_client.chat,\\\\n                model=stage.assigned_model or \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                prompt=prompt,\\\\n            )\\\\n            duration = time.time() - started_at\\\\n            return StageExecutionResult(\\\\n                stage_id=stage.stage_id,\\\\n                status=StageStatus.DONE,\\\\n                raw_output=raw_output,\\\\n                summary=self._summarize(raw_output),\\\\n                duration_sec=duration,\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n        if provider == \\\\\\\"github\\\\\\\":\\\\n            return await self._run_github_ops(stage, prompt, cwd, started_at)\\\\n\\\\n        command = self.build_command(stage, prompt, cwd)\\\\n        completed = await self._run_checked_command(\\\\n            command,\\\\n            cwd,\\\\n            context=f\\\\\\\"stage={stage.stage_type} role={stage.model_role}\\\\\\\",\\\\n        )\\\\n        combined_output = completed.stdout.strip() or completed.stderr.strip()\\\\n\\\\n        if self._looks_like_quota_exhaustion(completed.stdout, completed.stderr):\\\\n            raise QuotaExhaustedError(provider or \\\\\\\"unknown\\\\\\\", combined_output, partial_output=completed.stdout)\\\\n        if completed.returncode != 0:\\\\n            raise StageExecutionError(combined_output or f\\\\\\\"Stage failed with exit code {completed.returncode}\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n        duration = time.time() - started_at\\\\n        files_changed = self._list_changed_files(cwd)\\\\n        raw_output = self._normalize_output(completed.stdout, stage.assigned_provider)\\\\n        return StageExecutionResult(\\\\n            stage_id=stage.stage_id,\\\\n            status=StageStatus.DONE,\\\\n            raw_output=raw_output,\\\\n            summary=self._summarize(raw_output),\\\\n            files_changed=files_changed,\\\\n            duration_sec=duration,\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n    def build_command(self, stage: StageRecord, prompt: str, working_dir: str) -> list[str]:\\\\n        provider = stage.assigned_provider or self._infer_provider(stage.assigned_model or \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\")\\\\n        model = stage.assigned_model or \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        if provider == \\\\\\\"claude\\\\\\\":\\\\n            return [\\\\n                \\\\\\\"claude\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"-p\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"--output-format\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"stream-json\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"--model\\\\\\\",\\\\n                model,\\\\n                \\\\\\\"--allowedTools\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"Edit,Read,Bash,Grep,Glob,Write\\\\\\\",\\\\n                prompt,\\\\n            ]\\\\n        if provider == \\\\\\\"codex\\\\\\\":\\\\n            cmd = [\\\\n                \\\\\\\"codex\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"exec\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"--json\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"-C\\\\\\\",\\\\n                working_dir,\\\\n                \\\\\\\"--full-auto\\\\\\\",\\\\n                prompt,\\\\n            ]\\\\n            return cmd\\\\n        if provider == \\\\\\\"google\\\\\\\":\\\\n            return [\\\\n                \\\\\\\"gemini\\\\\\\",\\\\n                \\\\\\\"--model\\\\\\\",\\\\n                model,\\\\n                \\\\\\\"-p\\\\\\\",\\\\n                prompt,\\\\n            ]\\\\n        raise StageExecutionError(f\\\\\\\"Unsupported provider for CLI execution: {provider}\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n    async def run_verify_cmd(self, verify_cmd: str, working_dir: str | None) -> CommandExecution:\\\\n        return await self._run_checked_command(\\\\n            [\\\\\\\"bash\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"-lc\\\\\\\", verify_cmd],\\\\n            working_dir or \\\\\\\".\\\\\\\",\\\\n            context=\\\\\\\"verification command\\\\\\\",\\\\n            display_command=verify_cmd,\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n    async def _run_github_ops(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        stage: StageRecord,\\\\n        prompt: str,\\\\n        working_dir: str,\\\\n        started_at: float,\\\\n    ) -> StageExecutionResult:\\\\n        commands = [str(item) for item in stage.metadata.get(\\\\\\\"commands\\\\\\\", [])]\\\\n        if not commands:\\\\n            summary = \\\\\\\"GitHub stage had no commands configured.\\\\\\\"\\\\n            return StageExecutionResult(\\\\n                stage_id=stage.stage_id,\\\\n                status=StageStatus.DONE,\\\\n                raw_output=summary,\\\\n                summary=summary,\\\\n                duration_sec=time.time() - started_at,\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n        outputs: list[str] = []\\\\n        for raw_command in commands:\\\\n            rendered = raw_command.format(\\\\n                summary=stage.result_summary or \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                stage_type=stage.stage_type,\\\\n                working_dir=working_dir,\\\\n                prompt=prompt,\\\\n            )\\\\n            completed = await self._run_checked_command(\\\\n                [\\\\\\\"bash\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"-lc\\\\\\\", rendered],\\\\n                working_dir,\\\\n                context=f\\\\\\\"github_ops stage={stage.stage_type}\\\\\\\",\\\\n                display_command=rendered,\\\\n            )\\\\n            if completed.returncode != 0:\\\\n                raise StageExecutionError(completed.stderr.strip() or completed.stdout.strip() or rendered)\\\\n            output = completed.stdout.strip() or completed.stderr.strip() or f\\\\\\\"Executed: {rendered}\\\\\\\"\\\\n            outputs.append(output)\\\\n\\\\n        raw_output = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\".join(outputs).strip()\\\\n        return StageExecutionResult(\\\\n            stage_id=stage.stage_id,\\\\n            status=StageStatus.DONE,\\\\n            raw_output=raw_output,\\\\n            summary=self._summarize(raw_output),\\\\n            files_changed=self._list_changed_files(working_dir),\\\\n            duration_sec=time.time() - started_at,\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n    async def _run_checked_command(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        command: list[str],\\\\n        cwd: str,\\\\n        *,\\\\n        context: str,\\\\n        display_command: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n    ) -> CommandExecution:\\\\n        command_text = display_command or self._display_command(command)\\\\n        decision = await self.permission_gate.decide(\\\\n            command_text,\\\\n            context=context,\\\\n            available_models=self._safe_list_available_models(),\\\\n        )\\\\n        if decision.decision not in self.APPROVED_DECISIONS:\\\\n            raise PermissionBlockedError(command_text, decision)\\\\n        return await self.runner(command, cwd)\\\\n\\\\n    async def _run_command(self, command: list[str], cwd: str) -> CommandExecution:\\\\n        process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\\\\n            *command,\\\\n            cwd=cwd,\\\\n            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\\\\n            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\\\\n        )\\\\n        stdout, stderr = await process.communicate()\\\\n        return CommandExecution(\\\\n            returncode=process.returncode,\\\\n            stdout=stdout.decode(\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\", errors=\\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"),\\\\n            stderr=stderr.decode(\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\", errors=\\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"),\\\\n        )\\\\n\\\\n    def _normalize_output(self, stdout: str, provider: Optional[str]) -> str:\\\\n        text = stdout.strip()\\\\n        if provider != \\\\\\\"codex\\\\\\\" or not text:\\\\n            return text\\\\n        try:\\\\n            parsed = json.loads(text)\\\\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\\\\n            return text\\\\n        if isinstance(parsed, dict):\\\\n            if \\\\\\\"output_text\\\\\\\" in parsed and isinstance(parsed[\\\\\\\"output_text\\\\\\\"], str):\\\\n                return parsed[\\\\\\\"output_text\\\\\\\"]\\\\n            if \\\\\\\"message\\\\\\\" in parsed and isinstance(parsed[\\\\\\\"message\\\\\\\"], str):\\\\n                return parsed[\\\\\\\"message\\\\\\\"]\\\\n        return text\\\\n\\\\n    def _summarize(self, text: str) -> str:\\\\n        cleaned = \\\\\\\" \\\\\\\".join(text.split())\\\\n        if len(cleaned) <= 280:\\\\n            return cleaned\\\\n        return cleaned[:277] + \\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def _infer_provider(self, model: str) -> str:\\\\n        if model.startswith(\\\\\\\"claude-\\\\\\\"):\\\\n            return \\\\\\\"claude\\\\\\\"\\\\n        if \\\\\\\"codex\\\\\\\" in model:\\\\n            return \\\\\\\"codex\\\\\\\"\\\\n        if model.startswith(\\\\\\\"gemini-\\\\\\\"):\\\\n            return \\\\\\\"google\\\\\\\"\\\\n        if model.startswith(\\\\\\\"gpt-4o\\\\\\\") or model.startswith(\\\\\\\"gpt-4.1\\\\\\\"):\\\\n            return \\\\\\\"github\\\\\\\"\\\\n        return \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def _looks_like_quota_exhaustion(self, stdout: str, stderr: str) -> bool:\\\\n        text = f\\\\\\\"{stdout}\\\\\\\\n{stderr}\\\\\\\".lower()\\\\n        return any(token in text for token in (\\\\\\\"rate limit\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"quota\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"limit reached\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"exhausted\\\\\\\"))\\\\n\\\\n    def _list_changed_files(self, working_dir: str) -> list[str]:\\\\n        try:\\\\n            completed = subprocess.run(\\\\n                [\\\\\\\"git\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"diff\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"--name-only\\\\\\\"],\\\\n                cwd=working_dir,\\\\n                capture_output=True,\\\\n                text=True,\\\\n                check=False,\\\\n            )\\\\n        except OSError:\\\\n            return []\\\\n        if completed.returncode != 0:\\\\n            return []\\\\n        return [line for line in completed.stdout.splitlines() if line.strip()]\\\\n\\\\n    def _safe_list_available_models(self) -> list[str] | None:\\\\n        try:\\\\n            return self.chat_client.list_models()\\\\n        except Exception:\\\\n            return None\\\\n\\\\n    def _display_command(self, command: list[str]) -> str:\\\\n        return subprocess.list2cmdline(command)\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_15\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n@dataclass(slots=True)\\\\nclass PermissionDecision:\\\\n    risk_level: RiskLevel\\\\n    decision: str\\\\n    reason: str\\\\n    requires_voting: bool = False\\\\n -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,360p' harness/db.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"SQLite schema and connection helpers for the harness.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport sqlite3\\\\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\nfrom typing import Iterator\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nSCHEMA = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nPRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (\\\\n    task_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    title TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    description TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    goal TEXT,\\\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\\\n    complexity TEXT DEFAULT 'medium',\\\\n    priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,\\\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\\\n    working_dir TEXT,\\\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stages (\\\\n    stage_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(task_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\n    stage_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    stage_order INTEGER NOT NULL,\\\\n    model_role TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    assigned_model TEXT,\\\\n    assigned_provider TEXT,\\\\n    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',\\\\n    handoff_doc_path TEXT,\\\\n    result_summary TEXT,\\\\n    token_used INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    duration_sec REAL DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    started_at TEXT,\\\\n    finished_at TEXT,\\\\n    retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\n    verify_cmd TEXT,\\\\n    metadata TEXT DEFAULT '{}'\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS permission_requests (\\\\n    request_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    stage_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES stages(stage_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\n    action TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    context TEXT,\\\\n    risk_level TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    decision TEXT,\\\\n    voters TEXT DEFAULT '[]',\\\\n    decided_at TEXT\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS quota_events (\\\\n    event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    provider TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    account_email TEXT,\\\\n    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\n    details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',\\\\n    created_at TEXT NOT NULL\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status_priority\\\\n    ON tasks(status, priority DESC, created_at ASC);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_order\\\\n    ON stages(task_id, stage_order ASC);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stages_task_status\\\\n    ON stages(task_id, status, stage_order ASC);\\\\n\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass HarnessDB:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Small wrapper around a SQLite database file.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, db_path: str | Path) -> None:\\\\n        self.db_path = Path(db_path)\\\\n        self.db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\\\n        self.initialize()\\\\n\\\\n    def initialize(self) -> None:\\\\n        with self.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.executescript(SCHEMA)\\\\n\\\\n    @contextmanager\\\\n    def connect(self) -> Iterator[sqlite3.Connection]:\\\\n        conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)\\\\n        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row\\\\n        conn.execute(\\\\\\\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON\\\\\\\")\\\\n        try:\\\\n            yield conn\\\\n            conn.commit()\\\\n        except Exception:\\\\n            conn.rollback()\\\\n            raise\\\\n        finally:\\\\n            conn.close()\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_17\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,360p' harness/task_queue.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"SQLite-backed task and stage queue.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport json\\\\nimport uuid\\\\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\\\\nfrom typing import Iterable, Optional\\\\n\\\\nfrom harness.db import HarnessDB\\\\nfrom harness.models import (\\\\n    PermissionRequestRecord,\\\\n    PlannedStage,\\\\n    QuotaEventRecord,\\\\n    RiskLevel,\\\\n    StageRecord,\\\\n    StageStatus,\\\\n    TaskRecord,\\\\n    TaskStatus,\\\\n)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef utcnow_iso() -> str:\\\\n    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass TaskQueue:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"CRUD layer for tasks, stages, and audit tables.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, db: HarnessDB) -> None:\\\\n        self.db = db\\\\n\\\\n    def create_task(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        title: str,\\\\n        description: str,\\\\n        *,\\\\n        goal: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        verify_cmd: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        complexity: str = \\\\\\\"medium\\\\\\\",\\\\n        priority: int = 50,\\\\n        status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.PENDING,\\\\n        working_dir: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        task_id: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n    ) -> TaskRecord:\\\\n        now = utcnow_iso()\\\\n        record = TaskRecord(\\\\n            task_id=task_id or uuid.uuid4().hex,\\\\n            title=title,\\\\n            description=description,\\\\n            goal=goal,\\\\n            verify_cmd=verify_cmd,\\\\n            complexity=complexity,\\\\n            priority=priority,\\\\n            status=status,\\\\n            working_dir=working_dir,\\\\n            created_at=now,\\\\n            updated_at=now,\\\\n        )\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                INSERT INTO tasks (\\\\n                    task_id, title, description, goal, verify_cmd, complexity,\\\\n                    priority, status, working_dir, created_at, updated_at\\\\n                ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    record.task_id,\\\\n                    record.title,\\\\n                    record.description,\\\\n                    record.goal,\\\\n                    record.verify_cmd,\\\\n                    record.complexity,\\\\n                    record.priority,\\\\n                    record.status.value,\\\\n                    record.working_dir,\\\\n                    record.created_at,\\\\n                    record.updated_at,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            )\\\\n        return record\\\\n\\\\n    def get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\", (task_id,)).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def list_tasks(self, status: Optional[TaskStatus] = None) -> list[TaskRecord]:\\\\n        query = \\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM tasks\\\\\\\"\\\\n        params: tuple[object, ...] = ()\\\\n        if status is not None:\\\\n            query += \\\\\\\" WHERE status = ?\\\\\\\"\\\\n            params = (status.value,)\\\\n        query += \\\\\\\" ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC\\\\\\\"\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()\\\\n        return [self._row_to_task(row) for row in rows]\\\\n\\\\n    def pick_next_runnable_task(self) -> Optional[TaskRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                SELECT * FROM tasks\\\\n                WHERE status IN (?, ?, ?)\\\\n                ORDER BY\\\\n                    CASE status\\\\n                        WHEN ? THEN 0\\\\n                        WHEN ? THEN 1\\\\n                        ELSE 2\\\\n                    END,\\\\n                    priority DESC,\\\\n                    created_at ASC\\\\n                LIMIT 1\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PLANNING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.EXECUTING.value,\\\\n                    TaskStatus.PENDING.value,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            ).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_task(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def update_task_status(self, task_id: str, status: TaskStatus) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"UPDATE tasks SET status = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (status.value, utcnow_iso(), task_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def update_task_working_dir(self, task_id: str, working_dir: str) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"UPDATE tasks SET working_dir = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (working_dir, utcnow_iso(), task_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def save_stages(self, task_id: str, stages: Iterable[PlannedStage]) -> list[StageRecord]:\\\\n        now = utcnow_iso()\\\\n        records: list[StageRecord] = []\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            for planned in stages:\\\\n                record = StageRecord(\\\\n                    stage_id=uuid.uuid4().hex,\\\\n                    task_id=task_id,\\\\n                    stage_type=planned.stage_type,\\\\n                    stage_order=planned.stage_order,\\\\n                    model_role=planned.model_role,\\\\n                    assigned_model=None,\\\\n                    assigned_provider=None,\\\\n                    status=StageStatus.PENDING,\\\\n                    handoff_doc_path=None,\\\\n                    result_summary=None,\\\\n                    token_used=0,\\\\n                    duration_sec=0.0,\\\\n                    started_at=None,\\\\n                    finished_at=None,\\\\n                    retry_count=0,\\\\n                    verify_cmd=planned.verify_cmd,\\\\n                    metadata=dict(planned.metadata),\\\\n                )\\\\n                conn.execute(\\\\n                    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                    INSERT INTO stages (\\\\n                        stage_id, task_id, stage_type, stage_order, model_role,\\\\n                        assigned_model, assigned_provider, status, handoff_doc_path,\\\\n                        result_summary, token_used, duration_sec, started_at,\\\\n                        finished_at, retry_count, verify_cmd, metadata\\\\n                    ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\\\\n                    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                    (\\\\n                        record.stage_id,\\\\n                        record.task_id,\\\\n                        record.stage_type,\\\\n                        record.stage_order,\\\\n                        record.model_role,\\\\n                        record.assigned_model,\\\\n                        record.assigned_provider,\\\\n                        record.status.value,\\\\n                        record.handoff_doc_path,\\\\n                        record.result_summary,\\\\n                        record.token_used,\\\\n                        record.duration_sec,\\\\n                        record.started_at,\\\\n                        record.finished_at,\\\\n                        record.retry_count,\\\\n                        record.verify_cmd,\\\\n                        json.dumps(record.metadata),\\\\n                    ),\\\\n                )\\\\n                records.append(record)\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"UPDATE tasks SET updated_at = ? WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (now, task_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n        return records\\\\n\\\\n    def count_stages(self, task_id: str) -> int:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\\\\"SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM stages WHERE task_id = ?\\\\\\\", (task_id,)).fetchone()\\\\n        return int(row[\\\\\\\"count\\\\\\\"])\\\\n\\\\n    def get_stage(self, stage_id: str) -> Optional[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM stages WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\\\\", (stage_id,)).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_stage(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def list_stages(self, task_id: str) -> list[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM stages WHERE task_id = ? ORDER BY stage_order ASC\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (task_id,),\\\\n            ).fetchall()\\\\n        return [self._row_to_stage(row) for row in rows]\\\\n\\\\n    def list_completed_stages(self, task_id: str) -> list[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                SELECT * FROM stages\\\\n                WHERE task_id = ? AND status IN (?, ?)\\\\n                ORDER BY stage_order ASC\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (task_id, StageStatus.DONE.value, StageStatus.SKIPPED.value),\\\\n            ).fetchall()\\\\n        return [self._row_to_stage(row) for row in rows]\\\\n\\\\n    def next_pending_stage(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[StageRecord]:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            row = conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                SELECT * FROM stages\\\\n                WHERE task_id = ? AND status = ?\\\\n                ORDER BY stage_order ASC\\\\n                LIMIT 1\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (task_id, StageStatus.PENDING.value),\\\\n            ).fetchone()\\\\n        return self._row_to_stage(row) if row else None\\\\n\\\\n    def assign_stage_model(self, stage_id: str, model: str, provider: str) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET assigned_model = ?, assigned_provider = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (model, provider, stage_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def mark_stage_running(self, stage_id: str, handoff_doc_path: str) -> None:\\\\n        now = utcnow_iso()\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, handoff_doc_path = ?, started_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (StageStatus.RUNNING.value, handoff_doc_path, now, stage_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def complete_stage(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        stage_id: str,\\\\n        *,\\\\n        result_summary: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n        token_used: int = 0,\\\\n        duration_sec: float = 0.0,\\\\n    ) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, token_used = ?, duration_sec = ?,\\\\n                    finished_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    StageStatus.DONE.value,\\\\n                    result_summary,\\\\n                    token_used,\\\\n                    duration_sec,\\\\n                    utcnow_iso(),\\\\n                    stage_id,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def skip_stage(self, stage_id: str, *, result_summary: Optional[str] = None) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, finished_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (StageStatus.SKIPPED.value, result_summary, utcnow_iso(), stage_id),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def reset_stage_to_pending(self, stage_id: str, *, retry_count: Optional[int] = None) -> None:\\\\n        query = \\\\\\\"UPDATE stages SET status = ?, started_at = NULL\\\\\\\"\\\\n        params: list[object] = [StageStatus.PENDING.value]\\\\n        if retry_count is not None:\\\\n            query += \\\\\\\", retry_count = ?\\\\\\\"\\\\n            params.append(retry_count)\\\\n        query += \\\\\\\" WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\\\\"\\\\n        params.append(stage_id)\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(query, tuple(params))\\\\n\\\\n    def fail_stage(self, stage_id: str, *, result_summary: Optional[str], retry_count: int) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                UPDATE stages\\\\n                SET status = ?, result_summary = ?, retry_count = ?, finished_at = ?\\\\n                WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    StageStatus.FAILED.value,\\\\n                    result_summary,\\\\n                    retry_count,\\\\n                    utcnow_iso(),\\\\n                    stage_id,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def log_permission_request(self, record: PermissionRequestRecord) -> None:\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            conn.execute(\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n                INSERT INTO permission_requests (\\\\n                    request_id, stage_id, action, context, risk_level,\\\\n                    decision, voters, decided_at\\\\n                ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\\\\n                \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n                (\\\\n                    record.request_id,\\\\n                    record.stage_id,\\\\n                    record.action,\\\\n                    record.context,\\\\n                    record.risk_level.value,\\\\n                    record.decision,\\\\n                    json.dumps(record.voters),\\\\n                    record.decided_at,\\\\n                ),\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    def list_permission_requests(self, stage_id: Optional[str] = None) -> list[PermissionRequestRecord]:\\\\n        query = \\\\\\\"SELECT * FROM permission_requests\\\\\\\"\\\\n        params: tuple[object, ...] = ()\\\\n        if stage_id is not None:\\\\n            query += \\\\\\\" WHERE stage_id = ?\\\\\\\"\\\\n            params = (stage_id,)\\\\n        query += \\\\\\\" ORDER BY decided_at ASC, request_id ASC\\\\\\\"\\\\n        with self.db.connect() as conn:\\\\n            rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()\\\\n        records: list[PermissionRequestRecord] = harness.task_queue import TaskQueue\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef test_task_queue_creates_and_orders_tasks(tmp_path) -> None:\\\\n    queue = TaskQueue(HarnessDB(tmp_path / \\\\\\\"harness.db\\\\\\\"))\\\\n\\\\n    low = queue.create_task(\\\\\\\"low\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"low priority task\\\\\\\", priority=10, status=TaskStatus.PENDING)\\\\n    high = queue.create_task(\\\\\\\"high\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"high priority task\\\\\\\", priority=90, status=TaskStatus.EXECUTING)\\\\n    queue.save_stages(high.task_id, [PlannedStage(stage_type=\\\\\\\"code\\\\\\\", stage_order=1, model_role=\\\\\\\"coder\\\\\\\")])\\\\n assert picked is not None\\\\n    assert picked.task_id == high.task_id\\\\n    assert queue.next_pending_stage(high.task_id).stage_type == \\\\\\\"code\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef test_task_queue_updates_stage_lifecycle(tmp_path) -> None:\\\\n    queue = TaskQueue(HarnessDB(tmp_path / \\\\\\\"harness.db\\\\\\\"))\\\\n    task = queue.create_task(\\\\\\\"task\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"desc\\\\\\\", status=TaskStatus.EXECUTING)\\\\n    [stage] = queue.save_stages(\\\\n        task.task_id,\\\\n        [\\\\n            PlannedStage(\\\\n                stage_type=\\\\\\\"code\\\\\\\",\\\\n                stage_order=1,\\\\n                model_role=\\\\\\\"coder\\\\\\\",\\\\n                verify_cmd=\\\\\\\"pytest tests/test_feature.py\\\\\\\",\\\\n            )\\\\n        ],\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    queue.assign_stage_model(stage.stage_id, \\\\\\\"gpt-5.4-codex\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"codex\\\\\\\")\\\\n    queue.mark_stage_running(stage.stage_id, \\\\\\\"/tmp/handoff.md\\\\\\\")\\\\n    queue.complete_stage(stage.stage_id, result_summary=\\\\\\\"Implemented feature.\\\\\\\", token_used=123, duration_sec=4.5)\\\\n\\\\n    loaded = queue.get_stage(stage.stage_id)\\\\n    assert loaded is not None\\\\n    assert loaded.assigned_model == \\\\\\\"gpt-5.4-codex\\\\\\\"\\\\n    assert loaded.handoff_doc_path == \\\\\\\"/tmp/handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\\n    assert loaded.result_summary == \\\\\\\"Implemented feature.\\\\\\\"\\\\n    assert loaded.token_used == 123\\\\n    assert loaded.duration_sec == -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' tests/harness/test_stage_executor.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"import asyncio\\\\n\\\\nfrom harness.models import PermissionDecision, RiskLevel, StageRecord, StageStatus\\\\nfrom harness.permission_gate import PermissionGate\\\\nfrom harness.stage_executor import CommandExecution, PermissionBlockedError, StageExecutor\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass DummyPermissionGate:\\\\n    def __init__(self, decision):\\\\n        self.decision = decision\\\\n\\\\n    async def decide(self, command, **kwargs):\\\\n        return self.decision\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass DummyVoting:\\\\n    def __init__(self, decision):\\\\n        self.decision = decision\\\\n\\\\n    async def vote(self, **kwargs):\\\\n        return self.decision\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nasync def _runner(command, cwd):\\\\n    return CommandExecution(returncode=0, stdout=\\\\\\\"ok\\\\\\\", stderr=\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef make_stage(provider: str, metadata=None) -> StageRecord:\\\\n    return StageRecord(\\\\n        stage_id=\\\\\\\"stage\\\\\\\",\\\\n        task_id=\\\\\\\"task\\\\\\\",\\\\n        stage_type=\\\\\\\"github_ops\\\\\\\" if provider == \\\\\\\"github\\\\\\\" else \\\\\\\"code\\\\\\\",\\\\n        stage_order=1,\\\\n        model_role=\\\\\\\"github_ops\\\\\\\" if provider == \\\\\\\"github\\\\\\\" else \\\\\\\"coder\\\\\\\",\\\\n        assigned_model=\\\\\\\"gpt-4o\\\\\\\" if provider == \\\\\\\"github\\\\\\\" else \\\\\\\"gpt-5.4-codex\\\\\\\",\\\\n        assigned_provider=provider,\\\\n        status=StageStatus.PENDING,\\\\n        handoff_doc_path=None,\\\\n        result_summary=None,\\\\n        token_used=0,\\\\n        duration_sec=0.0,\\\\n        started_at=None,\\\\n        finished_at=None,\\\\n        metadata=metadata or {},\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef test_stage_executor_runs_github_commands(tmp_path) -> None:\\\\n    handoff = tmp_path / \\\\\\\"handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\\n    handoff.write_text(\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\", encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\")\\\\n    executor = StageExecutor(\\\\n        permission_gate=DummyPermissionGate(\\\\n            PermissionDecision(risk_level=RiskLevel.MODERATE, decision=\\\\\\\"auto_approved\\\\\\\", reason=\\\\\\\"ok\\\\\\\")\\\\n        ),\\\\n        runner=_runner,\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    result = asyncio.run(\\\\n        executor.execute(\\\\n            make_stage(\\\\\\\"github\\\\\\\", {\\\\\\\"commands\\\\\\\": [\\\\\\\"echo pushed\\\\\\\"]}),\\\\n            str(handoff),\\\\n            str(tmp_path),\\\\n        )\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    assert result.status == StageStatus.DONE\\\\n    assert result.summary == \\\\\\\"ok\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef test_stage_executor_blocks_unapproved_command(tmp_path) -> None:\\\\n    handoff = tmp_path / \\\\\\\"handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\\n    handoff.write_text(\\\\\\\"hello\\\\\\\", encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\")\\\\n    executor = StageExecutor(\\\\n        permission_gate=DummyPermissionGate(\\\\n            PermissionDecision(\\\\n                risk_level=RiskLevel.DANGEROUS,\\\\n                decision=\\\\\\\"needs_user_approval\\\\\\\",\\\\n                reason=\\\\\\\"blocked\\\\\\\",\\\\n            )\\\\n        ),\\\\n        runner=_runner,\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    try:\\\\n        asyncio.run(executor.run_verify_cmd(\\\\\\\"git push origin main\\\\\\\", str(tmp_path)))\\\\n    except PermissionBlockedError as exc:\\\\n        assert \\\\\\\"blocked\\\\\\\" in str(exc)\\\\n        assert \\\\\\\"git push origin main\\\\\\\" in exc.command\\\\n    else:\\\\n        raise AssertionError(\\\\\\\"Expected PermissionBlockedError\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef test_stage_executor_checks_raw_verify_command(tmp_path) -> None:\\\\n    from harness.models import ModelVote, VoteResult\\\\n\\\\n    async def user_approver(command: str, context: str):\\\\n        return False\\\\n\\\\n    executor = StageExecutor(\\\\n        permission_gate=PermissionGate(\\\\n            voting=DummyVoting(\\\\n                VoteResult(\\\\n                    decision=\\\\\\\"REJECT\\\\\\\",\\\\n                    votes=[ModelVote(model=\\\\\\\"gpt-5.4-codex\\\\\\\", vote=\\\\\\\"REJECT\\\\\\\", workflows.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport argparse\\\\nimport asyncio\\\\nimport json\\\\nimport logging\\\\nimport re\\\\nimport sys\\\\nimport time\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\n\\\\nimport uuid\\\\nimport yaml\\\\n\\\\n# Fix path to allow importing from the project root if run from anywhere\\\\nsys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.absolute()))\\\\n\\\\nfrom common.acp_client import ACPClient\\\\nfrom common.models import Message, MessagePart, RunStatus\\\\nfrom scripts.check_server_status import check_all_servers, StatusReport\\\\nfrom orchestrator.pipeline import PipelineDefinition, PipelineExecutor, PipelineError\\\\nfrom orchestrator.stage import StageDefinition, StageResult\\\\n\\\\n# Setup minimal logging, only warning+ so it doesn't clutter CLI\\\\nlogging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING, format=\\\\\\\"%(message)s\\\\\\\")\\\\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef hide_code_blocks(text: str) -> str:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Replaces markdown code blocks and diffs with a hidden placeholder.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    if not text:\\\\n        return \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    # Regex to match ```language \\\\\\\\n code... \\\\\\\\n ```\\\\n    pattern = re.compile(r\\\\\\\"```[a-zA-Z0-9_-]*\\\\\\\\n[\\\\\\\\s\\\\\\\\S]*?\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\", re.MULTILINE)\\\\n    return pattern.sub(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n[CODE DIFF/BLOCK HIDDEN]\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\", text)\\\\n    \\\\n\\\\nclass CLIExecutor(PipelineExecutor):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Custom executor that streams updates to the CLI interface and routes to ACP services.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    \\\\n    def __init__(self, pipeline_def: PipelineDefinition, config_path: str):\\\\n        super().__init__(pipeline_def)\\\\n        self.session_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]\\\\n        self.clients: dict[str, ACPClient] = {}\\\\n        \\\\n        with open(config_path, \\\\\\\"r\\\\\\\", encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\") as handle:\\\\n            data = yaml.safe_load(handle) or {}\\\\n            for name, info in data.get(\\\\\\\"agents\\\\\\\", {}).items():\\\\n                self.clients[name] = ACPClient(info[\\\\\\\"base_url\\\\\\\"])\\\\n                \\\\n    async def cleanup(self):\\\\n        import asyncio\\\\n        await asyncio.gather(*(client.close() for client in self.clients.values()), return_exceptions=True)\\\\n\\\\n    async def _invoke_model(self, stage_def: StageDefinition, prompt: str) -> str:\\\\n        agent_name = stage_def.metadata.get(\\\\\\\"agent\\\\\\\")\\\\n        if not agent_name:\\\\n            return await super()._invoke_model(stage_def, prompt)\\\\n            \\\\n        client = self.clients.get(agent_name)\\\\n        if not client:\\\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\\\\\"Unknown agent '{agent_name}' configured in metadata for stage {stage_def.stage_id}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        message = Message(\\\\n            role=\\\\\\\"user\\\\\\\",\\\\n            parts=[MessagePart(content=prompt)]\\\\n        )\\\\n        \\\\n        run = await client.create_run(agent_name, [message], session_id=self.session_id)\\\\n        if run.status != RunStatus.COMPLETED:\\\\n            raise RuntimeError(f\\\\\\\"Agent {agent_name} failed: {run.error}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        if not run.output_messages:\\\\n            return \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        return run.output_messages[0].text\\\\n    \\\\n    async def _execute_stage(self, stage_def: StageDefinition) -> StageResult:\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Override to provide CLI feedback before and after the stage.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n========================================================\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udd04 CURRENT STEP: {stage_def.name} ({stage_def.stage_id})\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\ud83e\\udd16 Agent Model:  {stage_def.model}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"========================================================\\\\\\\")\\\\n        \\\\n        # Prepare prompt to show user what is being asked\\\\n        prompt = self._prepare_prompt(stage_def)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n[Input] -> Sending to Agent:\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n        # We don't hide code blocks in prompt for context, but we can limit size\\\\n        short_prompt = prompt if len(prompt) < 1000 else prompt[:500] + \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n...[TRUNCATED FOR DISPLAY]...\\\\\\\" + prompt[-500:]\\\\n        print(short_prompt)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u23f3 Waiting for agent response...\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\")\\\\n        \\\\n        start_time = time.time()\\\\n        try:\\\\n            output = await self._invoke_model(stage_def, prompt)\\\\n            execution_time = time.time() - start_time\\\\n            \\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u2705 Agent responded in {execution_time:.1f}s\\\\\\\")\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n[Output] <- Received from Agent:\\\\\\\")\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n            \\\\n            # Hide code blocks for clean UI\\\\n            clean_output = hide_code_blocks(output)\\\\n            print(clean_output)\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\" * 40)\\\\n            \\\\n            return StageResult(\\\\n                stage_id=stage_def.stage_id,\\\\n                status=\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\",\\\\n                output=output,\\\\n                raw_output=output,\\\\n                execution_time=execution_time,\\\\n                model_used=stage_def.model,\\\\n            )\\\\n            \\\\n        except Exception as exc:\\\\n            execution_time = time.time() - start_time\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Stage failed after {execution_time:.1f}s: {exc}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            raise  # Let the base class intercept it and handle fail_fast\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nasync def ensure_servers_running(config_path: str, timeout: float = 3.0) -> bool:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Check if required servers are healthy. Return True if all ok.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udd0d Checking ACP Server Status...\\\\\\\")\\\\n    report = await check_all_servers(config_path, timeout)\\\\n    if report.unhealthy_count == 0:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\u2705 All {report.total_servers} servers are healthy!\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return True\\\\n    \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c SOME SERVERS ARE UNREACHABLE!\\\\\\\")\\\\n    for server in report.servers:\\\\n        if not server.healthy:\\\\n            err = f\\\\\\\" ({server.error})\\\\\\\" if server.error else \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"  - {server.base_url} is DOWN{err}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nPlease start the required servers (e.g. using scripts/run_*_server.py).\\\\\\\")\\\\n    return False\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef get_available_workflows() -> list[Path]:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Return a list of workflow YAML files.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    workflows_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent / \\\\\\\"workflows\\\\\\\"\\\\n    if not workflows_dir.exists():\\\\n        return []\\\\n    return sorted(workflows_dir.glob(\\\\\\\"*.yaml\\\\\\\"))\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nasync def main_async(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:\\\\n    # 1. Check Servers\\\\n    servers_ok = await ensure_servers_running(args.config, args.timeout)\\\\n    if not servers_ok:\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"               ORCHESTRA MULTI-AGENT CLI\\\\\\\")\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60 + \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    # 2. Select Workflow\\\\n    workflows = get_available_workflows()\\\\n    if not workflows:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\u274c No workflows found in the 'workflows/' directory.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    workflow_path = None\\\\n    if args.workflow:\\\\n        workflow_path = Path(args.workflow)\\\\n        if not workflow_path.exists():\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Workflow {workflow_path} not found.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            sys.exit(1)\\\\n    else:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"Available Workflows:\\\\\\\")\\\\n        for idx, wf in enumerate(workflows):\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"  [{idx + 1}] {wf.name}\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        while True:\\\\n            try:\\\\n                choice = input(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nSelect a workflow (1-%d): \\\\\\\" % len(workflows))\\\\n                idx = int(choice.strip()) - 1\\\\n                if 0 <= idx < len(workflows):\\\\n                    workflow_path = workflows[idx]\\\\n                    break\\\\n                else:\\\\n                    print(\\\\\\\"Invalid choice.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            except ValueError:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"Please enter a number.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nCanceled by user.\\\\\\\")\\\\n                sys.exit(0)\\\\n                \\\\n    print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u2705 Selected workflow: {workflow_path.name}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    # Load pipeline\\\\n    try:\\\\n        pipeline_def = PipelineDefinition(workflow_path)\\\\n    except PipelineError as e:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\u274c Failed to load pipeline: {e}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n        \\\\n    # 3. Get Task\\\\n    task_description = args.task\\\\n    if not task_description:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nEnter task description (press Enter twice to finish):\\\\\\\")\\\\n        lines = []\\\\n        while True:\\\\n            try:\\\\n                line = input()\\\\n                if not line and (not lines or not lines[-1]):\\\\n                    break\\\\n                lines.append(line)\\\\n            except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n                print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\nCanceled by user.\\\\\\\")\\\\n                sys.exit(0)\\\\n        task_description = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\".join(lines).strip()\\\\n        \\\\n    if not task_description:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\u274c Task description cannot be empty.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n\\\\n    # 4. Execute Pipeline\\\\n    print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\ud83d\\ude80 Starting Pipeline Execution...\\\\\\\")\\\\n    executor = CLIExecutor(pipeline_def, args.config)\\\\n    \\\\n    try:\\\\n        # Also map user_task so it's available for workflows explicitly requesting it\\\\n        initial_state = {\\\\\\\"user_task\\\\\\\": task_description}\\\\n        results = await executor.execute(task_description, initial_state=initial_state)\\\\n        \\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\" + \\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\ud83c\\udf89 PIPELINE EXECUTION COMPLETED\\\\\\\")\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"=\\\\\\\"*60)\\\\n        for stage_id, result in results.items():\\\\n            icon = \\\\\\\"\\u2705\\\\\\\" if result.status == \\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\" else \\\\\\\"\\u274c\\\\\\\"\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"{icon} Stage '{stage_id}': {result.status.upper()} ({result.execution_time:.1f}s)\\\\\\\")\\\\n            \\\\n        # Check for absolute failure\\\\n        if any(r.status == \\\\\\\"failed\\\\\\\" for r in results.values()):\\\\n            print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c Pipeline completed with ERRORS.\\\\\\\")\\\\n            sys.exit(1)\\\\n            \\\\n    except Exception as e:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\u274c Pipeline execution failed due to an error: {e}\\\\\\\")\\\\n        import traceback\\\\n        traceback.print_exc()\\\\n        sys.exit(1)\\\\n    finally:\\\\n        await executor.cleanup()\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef main() -> None:\\\\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\\\\\\\"Interactive Multi-Agent Orchestrator CLI\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--config\\\\\\\", default=\\\\\\\"config/agents.yaml\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Path to agents.yaml\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--timeout\\\\\\\", type=float, default=3.0, help=\\\\\\\"Per-server health check timeout in seconds\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--workflow\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Path to a specific workflow YAML, bypasses selection\\\\\\\")\\\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--task\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Initial task description, bypasses input dialog\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    args = parser.parse_args()\\\\n    \\\\n    try:\\\\n        asyncio.run(main_async(args))\\\\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExiting CLI...\\\\\\\")\\\\n        sys.exit(0)\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nif __name__ == \\\\\\\"__main__\\\\\\\":\\\\n    main()\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_24\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash harness/handoff.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_24\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,220p' harness/handoff.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Handoff document generation and artifact storage.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\nfrom typing import Iterable\\\\n\\\\nfrom harness.models import StageExecutionResult, StageRecord, TaskRecord\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass HandoffProtocol:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Persist stage inputs and outputs as markdown artifacts.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(self, artifact_root: str | Path = \\\\\\\"artifacts\\\\\\\") -> None:\\\\n        self.artifact_root = Path(artifact_root)\\\\n        self.artifact_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\\\n\\\\n    def task_dir(self, task_id: str) -> Path:\\\\n        path = self.artifact_root / task_id\\\\n        path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\\\n        return path\\\\n\\\\n    def handoff_path(self, task: TaskRecord, stage: StageRecord) -> Path:\\\\n        return self.task_dir(task.task_id) / f\\\\\\\"{stage.stage_order:02d}_{stage.stage_type}_handoff.md\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def output_path(self, task: TaskRecord, stage: StageRecord) -> Path:\\\\n        return self.task_dir(task.task_id) / f\\\\\\\"{stage.stage_order:02d}_{stage.stage_type}_output.md\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def generate(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        task: TaskRecord,\\\\n        stage: StageRecord,\\\\n        previous_stages: Iterable[StageRecord],\\\\n    ) -> str:\\\\n        previous = list(previous_stages)\\\\n        previous_stage_name = previous[-1].stage_type if previous else \\\\\\\"bootstrap\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n        lines = [\\\\n            f\\\\\\\"# Handoff: {previous_stage_name} -> {stage.stage_type}\\\\\\\",\\\\n            \\\\\\\"## Task\\\\\\\",\\\\n            task.title,\\\\n            \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\n            \\\\\\\"## Description\\\\\\\",\\\\n            task.description,\\\\n -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' models/codex/cli.py\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"from __future__ import annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport argparse\\\\nimport subprocess\\\\nimport sys\\\\nimport time\\\\nfrom concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed\\\\n\\\\nfrom . import account\\\\nfrom . import quota\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _format_bar(remaining_percent: int, width: int = 10) -> str:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Progress bar showing remaining quota (filled = remaining).\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n    remaining_percent = max(0, min(100, int(remaining_percent)))\\\\n    filled = int(round(remaining_percent / 100 * width))\\\\n    filled = max(0, min(width, filled))\\\\n    return \\\\\\\"[\\\\\\\" + (\\\\\\\"\\u2588\\\\\\\" * filled) + (\\\\\\\"\\u2591\\\\\\\" * (width - filled)) + \\\\\\\"]\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _format_reset(reset_at: int) -> str:\\\\n    if not reset_at:\\\\n        return \\\\\\\"n/a\\\\\\\"\\\\n    diff = max(0, int(reset_at) - int(time.time()))\\\\n    days = diff // 86400\\\\n    hours = (diff % 86400) // 3600\\\\n    return f\\\\\\\"{days}d {hours}h\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _fetch_quota_for_account(acc: account.CodexAccount) -> quota.CodexQuota:\\\\n    acc = account.refresh_account_tokens(acc)\\\\n    try:\\\\n        return quota.fetch_quota(acc.access_token, acc.account_id)\\\\n    except quota.QuotaError as exc:\\\\n        if exc.status_code == 403:\\\\n            acc = account.refresh_account_tokens(acc, force=True)\\\\n            return quota.fetch_quota(acc.access_token, acc.account_id)\\\\n        raise\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _fetch_quotas_parallel(\\\\n    accounts: list[account.CodexAccount],\\\\n) -> tuple[list[tuple[account.CodexAccount, quota.CodexQuota]], list[tuple[account.CodexAccount, Exception]]]:\\\\n    results: list[tuple[account.CodexAccount, quota.CodexQuota]] = []\\\\n    errors: list[tuple[account.CodexAccount, Exception]] = []\\\\n\\\\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=min(8, len(accounts))) as executor:\\\\n        futures = {executor.submit(_fetch_quota_for_account, acc): acc for acc in accounts}\\\\n        for future in as_completed(futures):\\\\n            acc = futures[future]\\\\n            try:\\\\n                results.append((acc, future.result()))\\\\n            except Exception as exc:\\\\n                errors.append((acc, exc))\\\\n\\\\n    return results, errors\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef _load_accounts_in_order() -> list[account.CodexAccount]:\\\\n    emails = account.list_accounts()\\\\n    accounts = []\\\\n    for email in emails:\\\\n        try:\\\\n            accounts.append(account.load_account(email))\\\\n        except FileNotFoundError:\\\\n            continue\\\\n    return accounts\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef cmd_status(_: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\\\\n    accounts = _load_accounts_in_order()\\\\n    if not accounts:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"No accounts found. Use `python -m models.codex.cli import` after logging in.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return 1\\\\n\\\\n    active_email = account.get_active_email()\\\\n    results, errors = _fetch_quotas_parallel(accounts)\\\\n\\\\n    quota_by_email: dict[str, quota.CodexQuota] = {}\\\\n    for acc, quo in results:\\\\n        quota_by_email[acc.email] = quo\\\\n        if quo.plan_type:\\\\n            acc.plan_type = quo.plan_type\\\\n        if quo.raw:\\\\n            acc.quota_snapshot = quo.raw\\\\n        account.save_account(acc)\\\\n\\\\n    errors_by_email = {acc.email: exc for acc, exc in errors}\\\\n\\\\n    max_email = max(len(acc.email) for acc in accounts)\\\\n    header = \\\\\\\"Codex Account Status\\\\\\\"\\\\n    print(header)\\\\n    print()\\\\n    print(\\\\n        f\\\\\\\"{'Account'.ljust(max_email)}  Plan   Remaining           Resets\\\\\\\"\\\\n    )\\\\n    print(\\\\n        f\\\\\\\"{'-' * max_email}  ----   -----------------  ------\\\\\\\"\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    for acc in accounts:\\\\n        marker = \\\\\\\">\\\\\\\" if acc.email == active_email else \\\\\\\" \\\\\\\"\\\\n        if acc.email in quota_by_email:\\\\n            quo = quota_by_email[acc.email]\\\\n            remaining = 100 - quo.weekly_used_percent\\\\n            bar = _format_bar(remaining)\\\\n            pct = f\\\\\\\"{remaining:>3}%\\\\\\\"\\\\n            reset = _format_reset(quo.weekly_reset_at)\\\\n            icon = \\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udfe2\\\\\\\" if remaining > 40 else \\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udfe1\\\\\\\" if remaining > 5 else \\\\\\\"\\ud83d\\udd34\\\\\\\"\\\\n            warn = \\\\\\\" \\u26a0\\\\\\\" if (quo.weekly_limit_reached or remaining <= 5) else \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n            plan = (quo.plan_type or acc.plan_type or \\\\\\\"unknown\\\\\\\").capitalize()\\\\n            print(\\\\n                f\\\\\\\"{marker} {icon} {acc.email.ljust(max_email)}  {plan.ljust(5)}  {bar} {pct}  {reset}{warn}\\\\\\\"\\\\n            )\\\\n        else:\\\\n            err = errors_by_email.get(acc.email)\\\\n            plan = (acc.plan_type or \\\\\\\"unknown\\\\\\\").capitalize()\\\\n            err_msg = str(err) if err else \\\\\\\"unknown error\\\\\\\"\\\\n            print(\\\\n                f\\\\\\\"{marker} {acc.email.ljust(max_email)}  {plan.ljust(5)}  error: {err_msg}\\\\\\\"\\\\n            )\\\\n\\\\n    return 0\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef cmd_list(_: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\\\\n    accounts = _load_accounts_in_order()\\\\n    active_email = account.get_active_email()\\\\n    for acc in accounts:\\\\n        marker = \\\\\\\">\\\\\\\" if acc.email == active_email else \\\\\\\" \\\\\\\"\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"{marker} {acc.email}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    return 0\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef cmd_switch(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\\\\n    target = account.set_active_account(args.email)\\\\n    print(f\\\\\\\"Active account set to {target.email}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    return 0\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef cmd_import(_: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\\\\n    acc = account.import_current_account()\\\\n    print(f\\\\\\\"Imported account {acc.email}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    return 0\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef cmd_login(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\\\\n    cmd = [\\\\\\\"codex\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"login\\\\\\\"]\\\\n    if args.device_auth:\\\\n        cmd.append(\\\\\\\"--device-auth\\\\\\\")\\\\n    try:\\\\n        subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)\\\\n    except FileNotFoundError as exc:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"codex CLI not found on PATH\\\\\\\", file=sys.stderr)\\\\n        return 1\\\\n    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as exc:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"codex login failed: {exc}\\\\\\\", file=sys.stderr)\\\\n        return exc.returncode or 1\\\\n    return 0\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef cmd_remove(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\\\\n    account.remove_account(args.email)\\\\n    print(f\\\\\\\"Removed account {args.email}\\\\\\\")\\\\n    return 0\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef cmd_rotate(_: argparse.Namespace) -> int:\\\\n    accounts = _load_accounts_in_order()\\\\n    if not accounts:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"No accounts found.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return 1\\\\n\\\\n    results, errors = _fetch_quotas_parallel(accounts)\\\\n    if errors:\\\\n        for acc, err in errors:\\\\n            print(f\\\\\\\"Warning: failed to fetch quota for {acc.email}: {err}\\\\\\\", file=sys.stderr)\\\\n\\\\n    candidates = [\\\\n        (acc, quo)\\\\n        for acc, quo in results\\\\n        if not quo.weekly_limit_reached and not acc.disabled\\\\n    ]\\\\n    if not candidates:\\\\n        print(\\\\\\\"All accounts are exhausted or disabled.\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return 1\\\\n\\\\n    best_acc, best_quota = min(candidates, key=lambda item: item[1].weekly_used_percent)\\\\n    active_email = account.get_active_email()\\\\n\\\\n    best_remaining = 100 - best_quota.weekly_used_percent\\\\n    if best_acc.email == active_email:\\\\n        print(f\\\\\\\"Already on best account {best_acc.email} ({best_remaining}% remaining).\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return 0\\\\n\\\\n    account.set_active_account(best_acc.email)\\\\n    print(\\\\n        f\\\\\\\"Switched to {best_acc.email} ({best_remaining}% remaining).\\\\\\\"\\\\n    )\\\\n    return 0\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:\\\\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\\\\\\\"Codex multi-account manager\\\\\\\")\\\\n    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest=\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\", required=True)\\\\n\\\\n    sub.add_parser(\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Show quota status for all accounts\\\\\\\")\\\\n    sub.add_parser(\\\\\\\"list\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"List accounts\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n    switch_parser = sub.add_parser(\\\\\\\"switch\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Switch active account\\\\\\\")\\\\n    switch_parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"email\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n    sub.add_parser(\\\\\\\"import\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Import current ~/.codex/auth.json\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n    login_parser = sub.add_parser(\\\\\\\"login\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Run codex login\\\\\\\")\\\\n    login_parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"--device-auth\\\\\\\", action=\\\\\\\"store_true\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n    remove_parser = sub.add_parser(\\\\\\\"remove\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Remove an account\\\\\\\")\\\\n    remove_parser.add_argument(\\\\\\\"email\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n    sub.add_parser(\\\\\\\"rotate\\\\\\\", help=\\\\\\\"Switch to account with most quota remaining\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n    return parser\\\\n\\\\n\\\\ndef main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:\\\\n    parser = build_parser()\\\\n    args = parser.parse_args(argv)\\\\n\\\\n    if args.command == \\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\":\\\\n        return cmd_status(args)\\\\n    if args.command == \\\\\\\"list\\\\\\\":\\\\n        return cmd_list(args)\\\\n    if args.command == \\\\\\\"switch\\\\\\\":\\\\n        return cmd_switch(args)\\\\n    if args.command == \\\\\\\"import\\\\\\\":\\\\n annotations\\\\n\\\\nimport json\\\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\\\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\\\\nfrom urllib import request\\\\n\\\\nimport yaml\\\\n\\\\nfrom config import settings\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nclass Chat2APIClient:\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Simple stdlib HTTP client for the local chat2api gateway.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n    def __init__(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        base_url: str = settings.CHAT2API_BASE_URL,\\\\n        timeout: float = settings.CHAT2API_TIMEOUT,\\\\n        catalog_path: str | Path = settings.HARNESS_ROLE_MODELS_PATH,\\\\n    ) -> None:\\\\n        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip(\\\\\\\"/\\\\\\\")\\\\n        self.timeout = timeout\\\\n        self._model_map = self._load_model_map(catalog_path)\\\\n\\\\n    @staticmethod\\\\n    def _load_model_map(catalog_path: str | Path) -> dict[str, str]:\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Build semantic-name \\u2192 chat2api_id mapping from role_models.yaml.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        try:\\\\n            with open(catalog_path, \\\\\\\"r\\\\\\\", encoding=\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\") as fh:\\\\n                catalog = yaml.safe_load(fh) or {}\\\\n        except (OSError, yaml.YAMLError):\\\\n            return {}\\\\n        return {\\\\n            name: info[\\\\\\\"chat2api_id\\\\\\\"]\\\\n            for name, info in catalog.get(\\\\\\\"models\\\\\\\", {}).items()\\\\n            if \\\\\\\"chat2api_id\\\\\\\" in info\\\\n        }\\\\n\\\\n    def resolve_model(self, model: str) -> str:\\\\n        \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Map a semantic model name to its chat2api ID, passthrough if unknown.\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n        return self._model_map.get(model, model)\\\\n\\\\n    def list_models(self) -> list[str]:\\\\n        payload = self._get_json(\\\\\\\"/v1/models\\\\\\\")\\\\n        return [item[\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\"] for item in payload.get(\\\\\\\"data\\\\\\\", []) if \\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\" in item]\\\\n\\\\n    def chat(\\\\n        self,\\\\n        *,\\\\n        model: str,\\\\n        prompt: str,\\\\n        system: Optional[str] = None,\\\\n    ) -> str:\\\\n        messages: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\\\\n        if system:\\\\n            messages.append({\\\\\\\"role\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"system\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\": system})\\\\n        messages.append({\\\\\\\"role\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"user\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\": prompt})\\\\n        payload = self._post_json(\\\\n            \\\\\\\"/v1/chat/completions\\\\\\\",\\\\n            {\\\\n                \\\\\\\"model\\\\\\\": self.resolve_model(model),\\\\n                \\\\\\\"messages\\\\\\\": messages,\\\\n            },\\\\n        )\\\\n        return payload[\\\\\\\"choices\\\\\\\"][0][\\\\\\\"message\\\\\\\"][\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\"]\\\\n\\\\n    def _get_json(self, path: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\\\\n        req = request.Request(f\\\\\\\"{self.base_url}{path}\\\\\\\", method=\\\\\\\"GET\\\\\\\")\\\\n        with request.urlopen(req, timeout=self.timeout) as response:\\\\n            return json.loads(response.read().decode(\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\"))\\\\n\\\\n    def _post_json(self, path: str, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\\\\n        data = json.dumps(body).encode(\\\\\\\"utf-8\\\\\\\")\\\\n        req = request.Request(\\\\n            f\\\\\\\"{self.base_url}{path}\\\\\\\",\\\\n            data=data,\\\\n            headers={\\\\\\\"Content-Type\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"application/json\\\\\\\"},\\\\n            method=\\\\\\\"POST\\\\\\\",\\\\n        )\\\\n        with request.urlopen(req, timeout=self.timeout) as response:\\\\n            return Context\\\\n- Complexity: complex\\\\n- Current Stage: code (coder)\\\\n- Working Directory: /home/ubuntu/scripts/code-orchestra\\\\n\\\\n## Previous Stage Output\\\\n### plan\\\\nImplement in three slices.\\\\n\\\\nSlice 1: keep routing pure, but add an explicit Codex account activation step before Codex execution so feasibility checks do not mutate global auth state.\\\\n\\\\nSlice 2: add a nohup-style daemon manager with PID metadata, stale PID detection, hidden worker entrypoint, and SIGTERM-driven graceful shutdown around `run_forever`.\\\\n\\\\nSlice 3: add centralized logging plus JSONL stage events, then build `dashboard` aggregates from DB state and event history instead of mixing metrics into ad hoc CLI output.\\\\n\\\\nBaseline before changes: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -q` => `21 passed`.\\\\n\\\\n## Current Stage Instructions\\\\nImplement all three features end-to-end. Keep the Codex account switch separate from pure model selection, prefer a portable nohup-style daemon path for tests, and add focused tests as each slice lands so the final `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v` run is stable.\\\\n\\\\n## Verification\\\\nRun: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.started\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_48\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,220p' artifacts/563b7352a8d94c82bf26787efb827fc0/01_plan_output.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":null,\\\"status\\\":\\\"in_progress\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_48\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,220p' artifacts/563b7352a8d94c82bf26787efb827fc0/01_plan_output.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Output: plan\\\\n## Summary\\\\nImplement the robustness work in three slices with tests after each slice:\\\\n\\\\n1. Codex account rotation\\\\n- Keep `QuotaRouter.select_model()` side-effect free so `can_run_stage()` remains safe.\\\\n- Extend routing to choose the best Codex account among all Codex quota snapshots using the existing use-it-or-lose-it scoring, and preserve the chosen `account_email` in `ModelChoice`.\\\\n- Add a separate activation step before Codex execution that calls `models.codex.account.set_active_account()` only when the chosen Codex account differs from the current active account.\\\\n- Log/surface the selected account in the routing reason and stage telemetry.\\\\n\\\\n2. Daemon lifecycle\\\\n- Add a small daemon manager module instead of embedding process control directly in CLI branches.\\\\n- Use a detached `subprocess.Popen(..., start_new_session=True)` worker for a portable nohup-style background process.\\\\n- Add `daemon start`, `daemon stop`, and `daemon status` CLI subcommands plus a hidden worker command that runs `Harness.run_forever()`.\\\\n- Persist daemon metadata in a PID file, detect stale PID files, and install a `SIGTERM` handler that lets the current iteration finish and exits cleanly.\\\\n\\\\n3. Logging, monitoring, and dashboard\\\\n- Add one centralized logging setup for the harness CLI/daemon path.\\\\n- Emit structured JSONL events for stage start, completion, retry, quota pause, permission pause, and failure.\\\\n- Compute dashboard aggregates from the DB plus the JSONL event stream: task counts by status, stage counts by status, total/average duration, total token usage, and execution error rate.\\\\n- Include daemon state, recent failures, and quota/permission pause counts in the dashboard output.\\\\n\\\\n## File Targets\\\\n- `harness/quota_router.py`\\\\n- `harness/main.py`\\\\n- `harness/stage_executor.py`\\\\n- `harness/task_queue.py`\\\\n- `config/settings.py`\\\\n- New modules are likely justified for daemon/process management and telemetry aggregation.\\\\n\\\\n## Test Plan\\\\n- Add router tests for multi-account Codex selection and no-side-effect feasibility checks.\\\\n- Add harness/main tests for Codex account activation before execution.\\\\n- Add daemon tests for PID lifecycle, stale PID detection, and stop/status behavior.\\\\n- Add telemetry/dashboard tests for JSONL event emission and aggregate summaries.\\\\n- Final verification: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -v`\\\\n\\\\n## Baseline\\\\nCurrent baseline is green: `python3 -m pytest tests/harness/ -q` passed with `21 passed`.\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_49\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\u89c4\\u5212\\u7ed3\\u679c\\u5df2\\u843d\\u5230
